title: Burnout Risk Check
description: Rate 30 recent work-life signals to understand your current burnout risk. This is not a clinical diagnosis.
instructions: Answer based on the last two to four weeks.
Choose frequency, not what you think should be true.
If your result feels serious or you feel unsafe, seek qualified support.

scaleQuestions.question: I feel emotionally drained before the workday even starts.
Small requests feel heavier than they should.
I need much longer than usual to recover after work.
I feel detached from work that used to matter to me.
I catch myself becoming more cynical or impatient.
It is hard to focus even on tasks I understand.
I postpone simple tasks because they feel mentally expensive.
My sleep, appetite, or body tension has been affected by work pressure.
I feel guilty when I rest.
I have less patience for people I care about after work.
I feel like no amount of effort is enough.
I avoid checking messages because I expect more demands.
My work feels misaligned with my values.
I feel trapped by responsibilities or expectations.
I have stopped feeling satisfied after completing work.
I recover energy through sleep, breaks, or weekends.
I can name what matters most this week.
I can say no or renegotiate when my workload is unrealistic.
I have someone I can speak honestly with about pressure.
I still experience moments of curiosity or enjoyment at work.
I can take breaks without feeling watched or punished.
My responsibilities are clear enough to prioritize.
I have enough control over how I do my work.
I can separate urgent work from important work.
I feel recognized for meaningful effort.
I feel pressure to be available outside reasonable hours.
I use distraction to avoid noticing how tired I am.
I feel numb rather than rested after downtime.
I worry that slowing down will cause everything to fall apart.
I feel less like myself because of work pressure.

LOW.title: Low Burnout Risk
LOW.summary: Your current answers suggest manageable pressure and several recovery resources.
weaknesses: Low risk is not the same as no stress

MODERATE.title: Moderate Burnout Risk
MODERATE.summary: Your result suggests stress is beginning to affect energy, focus, or emotional bandwidth.
strengths: There is still room to intervene early
weaknesses: Focus and patience may be more fragile
Rest may not fully restore you
relationships: Tell someone what pressure looks like for you.

HIGH.title: High Burnout Risk
HIGH.summary: Your answers show significant strain. You may need concrete workload, boundary, and support changes soon.
strengths: Naming the pattern is a strong first step
weaknesses: Cynicism or numbness may be increasing
careers: Clarify what can pause or move
relationships: Do not carry the pressure silently.
growthPlan: Talk to a manager, mentor, or professional
Plan real recovery, not only distraction

SEVERE.title: Severe Burnout Risk
SEVERE.summary: Your answers suggest intense strain and low recovery. Consider seeking qualified help and immediate practical support.
strengths: This result can justify asking for help
You do not need to solve it alone
weaknesses: Functioning may be sustained by adrenaline
Rest may no longer feel restorative
Health and relationships may be affected
careers: Discuss leave, coverage, or professional support
relationships: Tell a trusted person what is happening today.
growthPlan: Make a 24-hour safety and recovery plan
